    AI Overview non è disponibile per questa ricercaImpossibile generare un'overview dell'AI in questo momento. Riprova più tardi.
    AI Overview
    Mostra tutti
    Climate change is primarily driven by human activities, especially the burning of fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas. This releases heat-trapping greenhouse gases—such as carbon dioxide and methane—into the atmosphere, thickening the Earth's "blanket" and raising global temperatures. 
    The main drivers fall into these categories: 
    • Energy & Electricity: Burning fossil fuels to generate electricity and heat buildings accounts for the largest share of global emissions.
    • Transportation: Cars, trucks, ships, and planes run predominantly on fossil fuels, making the transport sector a massive contributor to global warming.
    • Industry & Manufacturing: Producing goods like cement, steel, and plastics releases significant amounts of carbon dioxide and other industrial pollutants.
    • Deforestation: Forests naturally absorb carbon dioxide. Cutting them down or clearing land diminishes this effect while releasing stored carbon back into the atmosphere.
    • Agriculture: Intensive farming and livestock rearing (e.g., cows digesting food) emit large amounts of methane and nitrous oxide. 
    To explore data-backed breakdowns and the latest scientific consensus, check out the NASA Science Causes of Climate Change overview or read the United Nations Climate Action guide.

    What are the 10 natural causes of climate change?
    Natural influences on the climate include volcanic eruptions, changes in the orbit of the Earth, and shifts in the Earth's crust (known as plate tectonics). Over the past one million years, the Earth has experienced a series of ice ages, including cooler periods (glacials) and warmer periods (interglacials).

    What is the main cause of climate change essay?
    Human activities such as deforestation, burning fossil fuels, farming livestock etc., generate an enormous amount of greenhouse gases. This results in the greenhouse effect and global warming which are the major causes of climate change.

    What are the 7 effects of climate change on the environment?
    Climate change affects the environment in many different ways, including rising temperatures, sea level rise, drought, flooding, and more. These events affect things that we depend upon and value, like water, energy, transportation, wildlife, agriculture, ecosystems, and human health.
